meta.description: Linux Log Guardian — Turkish-made open-source nginx WAF. log → ban in ~15 min.
hero.title: Linux Log Guardian
hero.tag: nginx access log → WAF/CRS → kernel ban · single chain · self-hosted
about.title: About — Linux Log Guardian
site.purpose: This is a public landing and download page. It serves static content: overview, install guide, evidence pack and contact. Log Guardian does not run here — you install it on your own Linux server.
about.body:
Linux Log Guardian is a open-source (MIT) self-hosted security stack built in Turkey. Instead of juggling Fail2ban, ModSecurity and separate scripts, you get one chain: read nginx logs → match OWASP CRS rules → ban at kernel level.

An independent community project — full code review, customization and on-prem hosting with no cloud lock-in.

  • Transparent source; community contributions welcome
  • Binary: log-guardian · package: log-guardian_*.deb
  • Core alone is production-ready (~15 min)
  • Dashboard, Grafana, fleet = optional Pro layer
flow.title: Single chain: log to kernel ban
flow.note: XDR, Wasm marketplace and LLM Copilot are long-term optional layers — Core alone is production-ready.
install.title: Setup guide (detailed)
install.intro: Step-by-step production setup on Ubuntu/Debian. Works without XDP on laptop or VirtualBox VM. Full docs: docs/QUICKSTART_NGINX.md and docs/LAPTOP_OPS.md.
install.req_body:
• Ubuntu 22.04 / 24.04 or Debian 12 (amd64)
  • nginx with writable access log
  • root/sudo (systemd, ipset, /etc/log-guardian)
  • Optional Pro: Docker (dashboard/Grafana), suitable kernel for eBPF
install.s1_body: Clone, build and run the main installer. install.sh sets up systemd units, rules and nginx log format.
install.block1:
git clone <repo> log-guardian && cd log-guardian
make -j$(nproc)
sudo bash install.sh
install.s2_body: Starts services and prepares API token sync for the dashboard.
install.block_api:
sudo bash scripts/install_first_run.sh
sudo bash scripts/ensure_api_security.sh
bash scripts/sync_dashboard_api_token.sh
install.s3_body: WAF needs the log_guardian format for request body and XFF. Installer usually applies it — verify:
install.block_nginx:
# examples/nginx-log-guardian.conf → site config
STRICT=1 bash scripts/check_nginx_log_format.sh
sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
install.s4_body: Check health, alarms and Prometheus metrics. post_install_verify.sh summarizes on laptop.
install.block_verify:
sudo log-guardian --health
sudo log-guardian --status
bash scripts/post_install_verify.sh
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:9091/metrics | head
install.s5_body: Without eBPF/XDP use --no-xdp with ipset bans. If service deps fail, run the repair script.
install.block2:
sudo bash install.sh --no-xdp
sudo bash scripts/install_first_run.sh
# if service FAIL:
sudo bash scripts/repair_no_xdp_stack.sh
install.tip: Tip: JWT setup: bash scripts/laptop_jwt_setup.sh. Quick demo: SKIP_WEBHOOK=1 bash scripts/demo_3min.sh
dashboard.title: Dashboard — after install (optional Pro)
dashboard.where: Important: The dashboard does not run on this marketing site. localhost:8443 is on your own Linux machine where you ran install — a visitor's browser localhost is not your server.
dashboard.body: The Pro dashboard (Next.js): fleet status, ban history, tests and evidence PDFs. Listens on :8443 with TLS on the host you installed. Harden with laptop_harden.sh before exposing to the internet.
dashboard.access_row1: Reads this page, downloads source or .deb — does not open port 8443.
dashboard.access_row2: After install.sh + dashboard_stack.sh on their Ubuntu/Debian host, opens https://localhost:8443 on that same machine.
dashboard.access_row3: SSH tunnel or (if firewall allows) https://SERVER_IP:8443. Do not expose with demo password.
dashboard.req_body:
• Core only needs nginx + sudo — dashboard not required
  • Dashboard needs Docker + dashboard_stack.sh
  • Grafana additionally grafana_stack.sh (optional)
dashboard.start:
# On the machine where you installed (not this website):
bash scripts/dashboard_stack.sh
# Browser on the same machine:
#   https://localhost:8443
# Login: admin / DASHBOARD_ADMIN_PASSWORD in .env
dashboard.features:
• Fleet: agent online/offline, recent alarms
  • Tests: phase100, competitive-proof, bench
  • Reports: PDF/JSON evidence pack
  • Grafana: tenant metrics via bash scripts/grafana_provision.sh
dashboard.optional: Is Core enough? Yes. If you only need log → WAF → kernel ban, skip the dashboard. CLI (log-guardian --status) and Prometheus (:9091/metrics) ship with Core.
dashboard.preview_note: Screenshots below are examples. Live UI runs only on your own installation.
dashboard.note: Summary: this site = download and learn. Dashboard = on your server, after install, optional.
warn.title: ⚠️ Before exposing to the internet
warn.p2: Public VPS: sudo env LG_NEW_PASSWORD='…' bash scripts/laptop_harden.sh
layers.title: Layers
layers.note: Pro and optional layers are add-ons; Core is enough for day-to-day use.
download.title: Download (.deb package)
download.intro: Build package with build_deb.sh. Integrity check without install: test_deb_local.sh.
evidence.title: Evidence pack
laptop.title: Laptop validation matrix
laptop.intro: Quick dev gate: sprint gate, 1h soak and 3 min demo. For 72h VM soak: laptop_soak_72h.sh --start.
laptop.block:
SKIP_PHASE100=1 bash scripts/laptop_sprint_gate.sh
SOAK_1H=1 bash scripts/laptop_soak_72h.sh --start
SKIP_WEBHOOK=1 bash scripts/demo_3min.sh
